name: Podio
access_notes: API access is free for all Podio accounts (both free and premium) and can be enabled by generating an API key from the user account settings page.
auth_notes: The primary authentication method is OAuth 2.0 (server-side/client-side flows). App-specific authentication using an app_id and app_token is also supported for automated scripts.
one_liner: Podio is an information management platform that provides a programmable REST API for integrating external services and building custom workflows on its core objects like workspaces, apps, and items.
mcp_notes: No official or well-known community Model Context Protocol server was found in the provided documentation.
api_notes: The API is a comprehensive RESTful interface covering virtually all Podio functionality, as the frontend itself is built upon it. Coverage includes applications, items, tasks, contacts, files, and more.

evidence.claim: The Podio API is free to use for both premium and free Podio accounts and users can generate API keys from their account settings.
The Podio API is RESTful, uses JSON, and requires OAuth2 for authorization.
The Podio API is a complete programmable interface to all Podio functionality.
Search citation: Podio API Key after leaving workspace
